parsed_review.summary:
CP00-249 (Risk A, 10 units, RP07.P04.M03.S09-S18) is a clean, internally consistent descriptor-only pack covering the RP07.P04 Primary Implementation Slice middle rows (permission badge, audit hint display, error message copy, responsive desktop/mobile layouts, keyboard/focus behavior, visual density check, synthetic fixture binding, build smoke, Hermes UI evidence). Independent read-only inspection confirms the production_ready candidacy is fully supported and the pack does NOT overclaim: manifest status is implemented_pending_review with production_ready:false, which is correct for this stage of the hardening sequence.

Coverage is exact: 10 RP07 units S09-S18, no duplicates, deliverable distribution {security_audit:2, implementation:3, ui:2, fixture:1, test:1, hermes_evidence:1} and micro_title {Primary Implementation Slice:10} match the prompt baseline, SEARCH_CORE_CP249_REQUIREMENTS (registry.js:1345), the manifest included_units, and the contract embedding. Descriptor-only + no-leak boundaries are enforced redundantly by validators.js (validateSearchCoreCp249UiSliceMidDescriptor), test/model.test.js, and a strict deepEqual between contracts/search-core-contract.json#ui_slice_mid_descriptor and the in-memory descriptor (validate-rp07-search-core-contract.mjs:733). Every row carries descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false, plus per-row guards (permission_decision_detail_included:false, audit_hint_detail_included:false, validation_error_detail_included:false, fixture_payload/real_client_data:false, executes_command_runtime:false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false).

Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true; no-write attestation has promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic evidence/review-packet handling (receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, H07 gate bound to validation commands), not product runtime. All no-write/no-real-data flags are false for every runtime/write surface (search/OCR/index/embedding/UI/command/object-storage/email/citation-ledger/loop/state/idempotency). index.js wildcard re-exports cover the 7 new CP249 public exports without modification. command-evidence.json records every expected validation command at exit 0, and the auth preflight is valid evidence (exit 0, 1450 bytes, well-formed JSON, ok:true) — not a CP178+ rejected form. Unrelated dirty files (the .docx and the c-ldip-01 LDIP review attempt) are correctly excluded from pack scope; the current new-session-handoff.md contains no embedded system-reminder injection vector. No P0/P1/P2 issues; one non-blocking P3 metadata imprecision. This review is independent verification supporting closeout; it is not final approval, which remains a human responsibility.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists the pack's own evidence directory under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: The pack's own evidence directory docs/closeout-packs/cp00-249/ (which holds manifest.json and command-evidence.json) appears in BOTH untracked_implementation_files (line 97) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 103). docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md is likewise dual-listed (lines 96 and 102). By contrast, the manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-249/manifest.json:523-526) correctly lists only the truly unrelated files (the .docx and docs/ldip-integration/claude-review-results/c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json). The changed-file-scope grouping is therefore broader/looser than the authoritative manifest list. This is a metadata-labeling imprecision only: the active_pack_implementation_refs (the 8 source files) are correct, active_pack_required_untracked_files is [], and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is []. It has no effect on coverage, descriptor-only/no-leak boundaries, authority boundary, or the production_ready determination.
findings.recommendation: Optional cleanup in the scope-generation step: exclude the pack's own evidence directory (and any genuine pack artifacts) from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved so it reflects only files outside pack scope, matching the manifest's worktree_transition_policy. No code or descriptor change required for CP00-249 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Informational metadata observation; does not affect CP00-249 production readiness, scope correctness, or closeout. Safe to defer or note in adjudication without remediation.
